titleStmt.title: Letter from William Logan to the Chief Secretary's Office, 5 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Logan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from William Logan regarding compensation for Court attendance. The author details the expenses which he incurred and expresses concern about a deduction from his expenses cheque. He requests the the deducted about be forwarded to him.
The letter includes an enclosure detailing his expenses.

correspAction.placeName: Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
160 North Street
Belfast 5th August 1916 Sir, As you will see by enclosed summons, I had to attend at the
Court for four days. I was living at Hillhead, Islandmagee, during the month of July
and had to come by rail each day to Belfast. I had almost two miles to come to the railway station, a car
would have cost me 2/- , but I got a seat on a car with
others at six pence each way. I did not include four pence each day for tram fare to and
from the station and Court-house, I travelled Third Class per rail,
I got some dinner where directed by Sargeant Mitchell, I did not
include any thing for the day I attended at the lower court on a
previous summons, yet when I got my expenses in the form of a cheque
payable at The Belfast Bank, there was 9/4 deducted, nad what for
I have no idea. I hope Sir, you will give the matter your favorable consider-
ation and instruct some one to pay me the balance due me. Yours respectfully, William Logan I aem.
II. Mrs. Moorhead,
Grown Societies,
For report
I.I.T.
7-8-16 I love
I 7/8
7/8
List of expenses as handed to Mr Mitchell.
26th July Railways fare, (third class) 2 3
portion of car fare from and
to the station 1 0
Dinner and tea 2 0
Wages 6 8
-----------
11-11
27th same as above 11-11
28th Railway fare 2 3
car fare 1 0
Dinner 1 4
Wages 6 8
29th Railway fare 2 3
car fare 1 0
Dinner 1 4
Wages 6 8
--------------
£ 2 -6 -4
By Cheque on a/o 1 -17 -0
---------------
BALANCE due me 9/4
----------------
